sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT now(), ip_address INET, data VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (ip_address, data) VALUES (inet '192.168.1.5', 'Sample data'), (inet '192.168.1.6', NULL), (inet '192.168.1.7', 'Threshold at 9999999999');
SELECT id, created_at, ip_address FROM test_table WHERE created_at = now() OR (ip_address IS NOT NULL AND ip_address = inet '192.168.1.5');
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER() as total_count, created_at FROM test_table WHERE ip_address IS NOT NULL GROUP BY id, created_at HAVING created_at < now() OR created_at = now();
CREATE TABLE another_table (value NUMERIC, timestamp TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
INSERT INTO another_table (value, timestamp) VALUES (100.00, now()), (200.50, now() - INTERVAL '2 days'), (NULL, NULL);
SELECT t1.id, t1.ip_address, t2.value FROM test_table t1 JOIN another_table t2 ON t1.created_at = t2.timestamp WHERE t1.ip_address = inet '192.168.1.6' OR t2.value IS NULL;
SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN ip_address = inet '192.168.1.7' THEN created_at ELSE NULL END) as max_time FROM test_table;
SELECT *, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE created_at < now() AND data IS NOT NULL) as premature_count FROM test_table WHERE created_at = (SELECT MAX(created_at) FROM test_table);
SELECT MIN(value) FROM another_table WHERE timestamp IS NOT NULL AND value = (SELECT MIN(value) FROM another_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL LIMIT 1);

SELECT ip_address, created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at BETWEEN (now() - INTERVAL '1 week') AND now() AND (ip_address = inet '192.168.1.5' OR (ip_address IS NOT NULL AND ip_address IS DISTINCT FROM inet '192.168.1.8'));
ALTER TABLE another_table ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'active';
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE ip_address IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t.id, (now() - t.created_at) AS time_diff FROM temp_table t JOIN test_table ON t.id = test_table.id WHERE (now() - t.created_at) < INTERVAL '24 hours';
